title: relay.rs creates a new reqwest::Client per CLI invocation
description: relay::add and relay::remove each call reqwest::Client::new() inline rather than accepting a client parameter or using the shared AppState client. This is only hit from the CLI so it has no runtime cost, but it is an inconsistency: every other outbound HTTP call uses a client with a 30s timeout and redirect::Policy::none() (SSRF defense). The relay CLI client inherits reqwest defaults: unlimited redirects and no timeout, meaning a malicious relay could hang the add command indefinitely or redirect to a private endpoint.
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment. CLI runs once and exits.
